bites.title: The Banking Regulation Act, 1949
bites.concept: The BR Act, 1949, was enacted to consolidate and amend the law relating to banking in India. It empowers RBI to license banks and regulate their management.

check_question.question: Which of these is the primary act regulating commercial banking operations in India?
check_question.explanation: The BR Act is the core operational law for banks.

bites.title: Types of Crossings
bites.concept: General crossing (parallel lines) ensures payment only through a bank. Special crossing (bank name included) ensures payment only through THAT bank.

check_question.question: A cheque with two parallel lines and 'ICICI Bank' written between them is a:
check_question.explanation: Including a bank name makes it a Special Crossing.

bites.title: SARFAESI Act - Basics
bites.concept: Securitisation and Reconstruction of Financial Assets and Enforcement of Security Interest Act allows banks to recover NPAs without court intervention.

check_question.question: SARFAESI enables recovery through:
check_question.explanation: It bypasses courts for secured assets.
